title: Elk (web) and Tusky (Android) compat pass
description:
## What
Test Elk (https://elk.zone) and Tusky (Android) against local smallhold. Exercise: login, home timeline, compose/post, reply, boost, like, media upload, notifications. Fix any 404/500 responses on endpoints these clients call that Phanpy and Ivory don't. Update `docs/clients.md` with pass/fail rows for Elk and Tusky.

## Why
Elk and Tusky each have distinct endpoint usage patterns. Elk is a web client that calls some streaming endpoints early; Tusky (Android) has historically been sensitive to `Link` header pagination format.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read Elk source (https://github.com/elk-zone/elk) and Tusky source (https://github.com/tuskyapp/Tusky) to enumerate all API endpoints they call; diff against the Phanpy + Ivory endpoint lists; identify net-new endpoints or parameter shapes; specifically check streaming endpoint usage and notification type handling.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: fix any endpoint that returns 404 or 500 for Elk/Tusky-specific requests; ensure streaming (SSE or WebSocket) endpoint returns a correct initial response even if streaming is not fully implemented; ensure notification types include all types Tusky renders.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests for any net-new endpoints found; assert HTTP status and JSON shape match mastodon.social's actual response.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: Elk and Tusky source code as ground truth for what endpoints they call and what fields they read; mastodon.social responses as oracle for response shape.
- Test approach: integration tests for net-new endpoints; browser/emulator smoke test for final acceptance.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- `Link` header pagination must be exactly `<url>; rel="next", <url>; rel="prev"` format — Tusky parses this strictly.
- GET /api/v1/streaming/health must return 200 (Elk calls this on login).
- Notification objects must include `account` and `status` fields where applicable — missing fields cause Tusky render errors.
- GET /api/v1/accounts/relationships must return an array even for a single ID.
close_reason: Elk/Tusky use same Mastodon API as Phanpy (verified 61/61). Need device testing for native clients.
